abuse_cases.expected_behavior: stale state; Top 3 not rendered; recompute command surfaced
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: covered by 'returns stale when artifact is older than threshold' test (24h default)

abuse_cases.expected_behavior: invalid_timestamp; loader does not fall back to mtime

abuse_cases.actual_behavior: loader returns future_timestamp with reason=generated_at_in_future_by_<seconds>s; tolerates skew under 5 minutes

abuse_cases.case: artifact copied from TLS path but stale
abuse_cases.expected_behavior: stale state on the fallback path
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: loader applies the same freshness gate to the fallback path; stale state returned

abuse_cases.case: top-level artifact fresh but TLS source stale
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: loader prefers PRIORITY_REPORT_PATH when present; only falls back to TLS sibling when top-level is missing. Operator should still run the recompute pipeline (Phase 2) to keep both paths in sync.
abuse_cases.status: ack — fallback only triggers on missing top-level

abuse_cases.case: priority entry points to non-registry ID
abuse_cases.expected_behavior: Top 3 card flagged as text-only; not rendered as graph node
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: extractTopThreeRecommendations marks is_registry_backed=false; warning surfaces text-only count; graph builder filters non-registry IDs

abuse_cases.case: priority entry points to roadmap label (TLS-BND-01)
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: registryContract.allowed_active_node_ids does not contain TLS-BND-* labels; partition function classifies them as non_registry

outcome: Phase 1 abuse cases are covered by tests in apps/dashboard-3ls/__tests__/lib/artifactLoaderPriority.test.ts and apps/dashboard-3ls/__tests__/lib/topThreeRecommendations.test.ts.
